evals.name: typescript-feature-with-tests
evals.prompt: Add a new authentication middleware to src/auth.ts that validates JWT tokens. The middleware should check the Authorization header, verify the token, and attach the user to the request object.
evals.expected_output: Skill explains TDD workflow: write failing test first, then implement. References tdd-guard-vitest or tdd-guard-jest setup. After implementation, mentions TS quality gate will run ESLint/Prettier checks.
expectations: Mentions writing a failing test before implementation
Mentions TypeScript quality gate runs after edit

evals.name: python-refactor-request
evals.prompt: Refactor the database connection pooling in db/connection.py to use async/await. Current implementation is blocking and causing performance issues.
evals.expected_output: Skill explains TDD + Python quality workflow: write failing test for async behavior, implement, then ruff/mypy will validate. Mentions auto-fix capabilities.

evals.name: quality-gate-error-fix
evals.prompt: I'm getting blocked by the quality gate with TypeScript errors. Here's the error: 'Type string is not assignable to type number'. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Skill explains quality gate error handling: read errors, apply auto-fix if available, manually fix type errors, gate re-runs automatically.
expectations: Explains how to read quality gate errors
Explains manual fix process for type errors
Notes gate re-runs on next edit

evals.name: partial-install-python-only
evals.prompt: I'm working on a Python-only project with pytest. What quality tools should I install?
evals.expected_output: Skill recommends Python-specific setup: tdd-guard-pytest for TDD, ruff + mypy for quality gate. Explains partial install workflow.

evals.name: tdd-guard-blocking-confusion
evals.prompt: Why am I getting 'No failing test found' when I try to edit src/service.ts? I just want to add a logging statement.
evals.expected_output: Skill explains TDD Guard purpose: enforce test-first development. Even small changes require a failing test. Suggests writing a test that verifies the logging behavior.
expectations: Explains TDD Guard blocks all implementation
Does not suggest bypassing the guard

evals.name: eslint-not-found-error
evals.prompt: The quality gate says 'ESLint not found' but I'm editing a TypeScript file. What do I do?
evals.expected_output: Skill explains ESLint is required for TS quality gate. Provides install command (npm install --save-dev eslint) or how to disable in hook-config.json.

evals.name: full-workflow-question
evals.prompt: Walk me through the complete workflow for adding a new feature to this TypeScript project.
evals.expected_output: Skill explains full pipeline: 1) Write failing test, 2) TDD Guard allows implementation, 3) Implement feature, 4) TS quality gate validates, 5) Fix any issues, 6) Commit.

evals.name: documentation-edit-exception
evals.prompt: I need to update the README.md with new API documentation. Will the quality gates block me?
evals.expected_output: Skill explains quality gates only apply to code files (.ts, .js, .py, etc.), not documentation. README edits proceed without TDD or linting checks.
expectations: Lists code file extensions that trigger gates

evals.name: mypy-type-errors
evals.prompt: Mypy is reporting 15 type errors in my Python code. Should I fix all of them before continuing?
evals.expected_output: Skill explains exit code 2 means blocking - all errors must be fixed. Suggests using mypy's output to prioritize, fix incrementally, gate re-runs on each edit.
expectations: Confirms all blocking errors must be fixed

evals.name: vitest-reporter-setup
evals.prompt: How do I set up the Vitest test reporter for TDD Guard?
evals.expected_output: Skill explains tdd-guard-vitest installation and vitest.config.ts configuration with VitestReporter and project root path.

evals.name: should-not-trigger-general-chat
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT trigger - this is general chat, not a code quality workflow question.

evals.name: should-not-trigger-unrelated-coding
evals.prompt: Write a Python script to scrape data from example.com and save it to CSV.
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT trigger strongly - this is a general coding task without quality gate context. May mention TDD as best practice but full skill not needed.
expectations: May mention testing as best practice

evals.name: should-not-trigger-math-question
evals.prompt: What's the time complexity of binary search?
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT trigger - this is a CS theory question, not about quality gates.

evals.name: edge-case-mixed-language-project
evals.prompt: I have a monorepo with both TypeScript backend and Python ML services. How do quality gates work?
evals.expected_output: Skill explains both gates can coexist: TS quality gate for backend files, PY quality gate for ML services. TDD Guard works with both via appropriate reporters.

evals.name: edge-case-auto-fix-verification
evals.prompt: The quality gate said it auto-fixed 3 issues but I still have 2 errors. What happened?
evals.expected_output: Skill explains auto-fix handles fixable issues (formatting, simple lint), but type errors and complex issues require manual fixes. Shows how to identify remaining issues.
